data.description:
## Why
The delivered epic (Gerrit stack 558/560/561/563/576/579/580/581) diverged from the
APPROVED epic design on authorship-payload format. bde5 (change 561) signs the
event's RAW canonical bytes; the approved AC requires an **in-toto Statement**
payload. This story transitions the delivered code to the approved design. (User
rejected the delivered design; full divergence record in session_log d7c0.)

## What
Rework `attest/authorship.py` so authorship signatures wrap an **in-toto Statement**:
- The Statement's `subject` binds `{event_uuid, content_hash}` (content_hash = a
  digest of the event's canonical content EXCLUDING `author_sig`).
- The Statement rides a DSSE envelope with
  `payloadType = application/vnd.rebar.authorship.v1+json`, signed over
  `PAE(payloadType, payload)` via the foundation SSHSIG scheme (substrate unchanged).
- `sign_authorship` builds the Statement + content_hash; `verify_authorship` parses
  the Statement, checks subject.event_uuid + content_hash match the event, THEN
  verifies the DSSE signature against the identity's in-band keys.
- The write seam (`_seam.py`) stamps `author_sig` = DSSE-encoded Statement envelope,
  hashing the canonical event minus author_sig.
- Add the epic-AC-named `create_placeholder(provider, external_id, display_name)`
  path (alias over the delivered `ensure_identity_for`) so the AC surface exists.

## Scope
In: the in-toto Statement authorship payload (sign/verify/content_hash) + author_sig
on the write seam + the create_placeholder alias. Out: era model (e165 transition)
and ledger/verdict (3183 transition), which CONSUME this story's content_hash + verify.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `sign_authorship` produces a DSSE envelope whose payload is an in-toto Statement
  (payloadType application/vnd.rebar.authorship.v1+json) with a subject binding
  `{event_uuid, content_hash}`; a test asserts the structure + payloadType.
- [ ] `verify_authorship` verifies an event signed by identity A against A's in-band
  keys; a test asserts it does NOT verify against identity B and FAILS on a tampered
  event (payload or content_hash mismatch).
- [ ] An unavailable signing key yields an `unsigned` (Tier-0) write, never a blocked
  write (test).
- [ ] `create_placeholder(provider, external_id, display_name)` mints a keyless
  placeholder identity (test).

## file_impact
- `src/rebar/attest/authorship.py`
- `src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py`
- `src/rebar/_commands/identity.py`
data.title: transition: authorship payload -> in-toto Statement (subject binds {event_uuid, content_hash})
